articles.title: Extreme heat to scorch Northern, Central regions - Bangkok Post
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Haryana
articles.full_text:
PUBLISHED : 6 Apr 2026 at 04:14
Temperatures could hit 42C this week as the Meteorological Department warns of scattered thunderstorms and gusty winds.
The Northern and Central regions are facing extreme heat this week, with daytime temperatures soaring to 42C and hazy skies, the Meteorological Department reported.
The heat, combined with southern and western winds, is creating scattered thunderstorms and occasional gusty winds across some areas.
The Northeastern region is expected to reach highs of 37–41C, while the Central region may climb to 38–42C, with thunderstorms mostly affecting its southern parts.
Bangkok and surrounding provinces could see temperatures up to 41C, accompanied by isolated storms. In the Eastern and Southern regions, daytime heat will remain high, with rainfall in 10–30% of areas.
These conditions are expected to persist across northern, central and southern Thailand until Saturday.

articles.title: IMD issues orange alert for thunderstorms and hailstorm in J&K, Delhi, Punjab and Haryana - News On AIR
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Haryana
articles.full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an orange alert for thunderstorms accompanied with hailstorm over Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab and West Rajasthan for today. The Weather Agency has also predicted heavy rainfall over the North-Eastern region for the day.
Meanwhile, thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds have been predicted for Karnataka, Odisha, Chhattisgarh, Telangana, Uttarakhand and Madhya Pradesh till tomorrow. Similar conditions are expected over Andhra Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Jharkhand and West Bengal during the next two-three days.
